pattern: Since each shop must be bought from back to front, the actual per-shop ordering imposes no real constraint beyond 'ascending', so pairing every item's value against an ascending day counter by the rearrangement inequality just means sorting every value across every shop together.
hints: Within a single shop you are forced to buy in increasing order of value (the rightmost remaining item is always the current cheapest), so any global buying order that respects that per-shop order is achievable.

By the rearrangement inequality, the sum of day-times-value is maximized when both sequences increase together, so the globally optimal order is simply every item's value sorted from smallest to largest.
Flatten every shop's values into one list, sort it ascending, and pair position i (1-indexed) with day i; the per-shop increasing order is automatically preserved by a stable global sort.

edge_inputs.description: three shops of three items each
two shops of five items each
a single shop with a single item
a single shop with many items
duplicate values across different shops must still both be sold
every shop's cheapest item ties, forcing a stable tie-break

stress.description: the full 10-shop boundary with values at the top of the range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 10))
    n = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    values = []
    for _ in range(m):
        row = sorted((rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)), reverse=True)
        values.append(row)
    return [values]
stress.note: exercises totals large enough to approach the safe double-precision limit

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxSpending(self, values):
        # Independent implementation: simulate the actual day-by-day buying
        # process with a min-heap over each shop's current rightmost item,
        # instead of flattening and sorting everything up front.
        import heapq
        m = len(values)
        ptr = [len(row) - 1 for row in values]
        heap = []
        for i in range(m):
            heapq.heappush(heap, (values[i][ptr[i]], i))
        total = 0
        day = 1
        while heap:
            v, i = heapq.heappop(heap)
            total += v * day
            day += 1
            ptr[i] -= 1
            if ptr[i] >= 0:
                heapq.heappush(heap, (values[i][ptr[i]], i))
        return total
